subject: Public Health
title: Health and Migration
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Health and Migration.

hook.text: Migrants are often healthier than the populations they join—but their health deteriorates rapidly after arrival.

buildup.text: The 'healthy migrant effect' fades as barriers to healthcare, discrimination, and poor conditions take their toll.

discovery.text: Refugees face trauma, overcrowding, and limited access to care—creating unique and compounding health needs.

twist.text: Many countries exclude undocumented migrants from healthcare—pushing them into emergency rooms at higher cost.

climax.text: Inclusive health policies—covering all residents regardless of status—improve public health for everyone.

punchline.text: Excluding migrants from healthcare doesn't save money. It costs lives.

quiz.question: What is the 'healthy migrant effect'?
options: Migrants are initially healthier but their health deteriorates after arrival
Migrants spread diseases to host countries
Healthcare improves for everyone when migrants arrive
